nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to skill relevance
nodes.grounding_span: helps me complete projects much faster while maintaining a competitive high quality of work

nodes.label: Ethical boundary of AI use in creative work
nodes.grounding_span: I don't like to ask for full drafts because that's what I'm being paid to do and it feels unethical

nodes.label: AI as skill displacer in certain domains
nodes.label_negative: AI as skill enhancer in other domains
nodes.grounding_span: AI has already taken over in areas of my job and it feels pointless to maintain these skills

nodes.label: AI as assistant vs AI as editor
nodes.grounding_span: Sometimes I feel like I'm becoming the AI's assistant and editor instead of the AI being the assistant

nodes.label: Creative ownership and professional integrity
nodes.grounding_span: that's what I'm being paid to do and it feels unethical

nodes.label: Productivity and competitive advantage
nodes.grounding_span: helps me complete projects much faster while maintaining a competitive high quality of work

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for creative brainstorming under time pressure
nodes.grounding_span: AI helped me come up with creative hooks that made the process of writing much easier

nodes.label: Frustration with AI displacing valued skills
nodes.grounding_span: AI has already taken over in areas of my job and it feels pointless to maintain these skills

nodes.label: Concern about role reversal with AI
nodes.grounding_span: Sometimes I feel like I'm becoming the AI's assistant and editor instead of the AI being the assistant

nodes.label: Iterative collaborative refinement — writes first then seeks AI feedback
nodes.grounding_span: I like to write complete drafts myself and then ask AI for feedback or ways to improve

nodes.label: Strategic skill investment — prioritizes skills AI cannot replace
nodes.grounding_span: I think it's more worth investing in skills that AI isn't that good at
